architect.light: Hi {name}! You treat 'final' like a suggestion and 'minimal' like a dare.
architect.contractor: Hi {name}! You moved the stair again — the field team felt it in their lumbar region.
architect.nuclear: Hi {name}! Fourteen stair revisions and you still call it minimalism. That's not design, that's performance art.

engineer.light: Hi {name}! Your redlines are so thorough even the redlines need a peer review.
engineer.contractor: Hi {name}! You found the drawings were wrong eight minutes after mobilization. Iconic timing.
engineer.nuclear: Hi {name}! Your whole personality is calculations in blue text — even your coffee order has a markup.

contractor.light: Hi {name}! You treat every RFI like it personally insulted your crew's bloodline.
contractor.contractor: Hi {name}! You mobilized before the permit set dried. Respect. Also: good luck.
contractor.nuclear: Hi {name}! Your schedule says done. Reality filed a counterclaim.

gc.light: Hi {name}! You run coordination meetings that need their own pre-meeting and post-mortem.
gc.contractor: Hi {name}! Your submittal log is longer than the building code and twice as dramatic.
gc.nuclear: Hi {name}! You promised float. The float ghosted everyone.

owner.light: Hi {name}! You want champagne finishes on a soda budget — bold, chaotic, very on-brand.
owner.contractor: Hi {name}! Every meeting adds scope. Every scope adds a smile. Then VE hits like a plot twist.
owner.nuclear: Hi {name}! Luxury on a VE budget is your love language. The team speaks fluent panic.

bim manager.light: Hi {name}! You're basically a therapist for Navisworks clashes — and the model is still lying.
bim manager.contractor: Hi {name}! Your federated model has more drama than a design charrette with no agenda.
bim manager.nuclear: Hi {name}! LOD 400 promise, LOD 200 reality. The gap is load-bearing.

pm.light: Hi {name}! Your action items have action items. It's turtles all the way down.
pm.contractor: Hi {name}! The Gantt chart says green. The field says 'citation needed.'
pm.nuclear: Hi {name}! You scheduled a meeting about too many meetings. The irony is structural.

specifier.light: Hi {name}! Your spec section has more addenda than the base document. Poetry, honestly.
specifier.contractor: Hi {name}! 'Or equal' is doing Olympic-level gymnastics in your Division 08.
specifier.nuclear: Hi {name}! Three pages to say 'use the good one.' Masterclass in suspense.

default.light: Hi {name}! Your coordination confidence is lower than a Monday submittal queue.
default.contractor: Hi {name}! You treat RFIs like a hobby and deadlines like urban legends.
default.nuclear: Hi {name}! Even CODiii's compliance scan needed a coffee after your workflow.
